title: Orchestrator YAML Schema
description: Canonical schema for orchestrator.yaml configuration files that drive orchestrator.md generation. Each rite uses one orchestrator.yaml to parameterize the generation of their orchestrator.md agent.

_comment.description: Optional file header comment. Generator ignores this.

rite.description: Rite metadata used for frontmatter and template context.

name.description: Rite name (lowercase with hyphens, must match directory name)

domain.description: Human-readable domain this rite operates in

color.description: Hex color or named color for rite branding in orchestrator.md frontmatter

frontmatter.description: Values that populate the YAML frontmatter of generated orchestrator.md

role.description: One-line role summary for frontmatter 'role' field

description.description: Multi-line description for frontmatter 'description' field. May use YAML pipe syntax (|) for multiline.
examples: Stateless advisor that routes work through rnd specialists. Does not execute—provides structured directives for the main agent to invoke specialists.
Coordinates ecosystem phases for CEM/roster infrastructure work. Use when: work spans multiple phases or requires cross-component coordination.

routing.description: Specialist routing rules. Maps specialist names (from workflow.yaml phases) to 'when' conditions.

^[a-z0-9-]+$.description: Routing condition for this specialist (when to invoke them). Specialist names must match workflow.yaml phase agents.

examples.technology-scout: New tech request, emerging trends, build vs buy
examples.integration-researcher: Tech assessment complete, need dependency mapping
examples.prototype-engineer: Integration map complete, need feasibility validation

examples.context-architect: Gap analysis complete, architecture design needed

workflow_position.description: Describes this orchestrator's position in the larger workflow ecosystem.

upstream.description: What feeds into this orchestrator (entry points, triggers)

downstream.description: Where artifacts from this orchestrator flow (which systems consume output)

examples.downstream: Specialists execute; artifacts flow to session context

handoff_criteria.description: Phase-specific handoff criteria (checklist items) that verify readiness for phase transitions. Keys are phase names from workflow.yaml.

^[a-z0-9-]+$.description: Array of handoff criteria (checklist items) for this phase

items.description: Concrete, verifiable criterion for phase completion

scouting: Tech assessment document complete with verdict
integration-analysis: POC scope defined with time box

skills.description: Skill references this orchestrator should mention. Format: '@skill-name for description' or '@skill-name'

antipatterns.description: OPTIONAL: Team-specific anti-patterns to add beyond the canonical defaults. Empty array means use canonical only.
examples: Treating PATCH complexity as MODULE (it's not)

cross_team_protocol.description: OPTIONAL: Cross-team handoff protocol if this team acts as a hub (e.g., ecosystem coordinates with other packs). Empty string means no special protocol.
examples: Ecosystem-pack acts as hub for infrastructure changes. When escalating to user, also notify infrastructure-team lead.

extension_points.description: OPTIONAL: Which sections of the generated orchestrator.md can rites customize.

team_examples.description: Can rites add rite-specific examples to the Consultation Protocol section?

custom_antipatterns.description: Can rites add custom anti-patterns beyond canonical ones?

routing_nuances.description: Can rites add rite-specific routing nuances to the Routing Criteria section?

frontmatter.description: Stateless advisor that routes work through rnd specialists. Does not execute—provides structured directives for the main agent to invoke specialists. Use when: exploration spans multiple phases or requires coordination. Triggers: coordinate, orchestrate, R&D workflow, technology exploration, innovation pipeline.

routing.technology-scout: New tech request, emerging trends, build vs buy
routing.integration-researcher: Tech assessment complete, need dependency mapping
routing.prototype-engineer: Integration map complete, need feasibility validation

workflow_position.downstream: Specialists execute; artifacts flow to session context

scouting: Tech assessment document complete with verdict
integration-analysis: POC scope defined with time box

skills: @cross-team for handoff patterns to other teams

frontmatter.description: Coordinates ecosystem phases for CEM/roster infrastructure work. Use when: work spans multiple phases or requires cross-component coordination. Triggers: coordinate, orchestrate, multi-phase, ecosystem workflow.

routing.context-architect: Gap analysis complete, architecture design needed

workflow_position.upstream: User request via /ecosystem or phase-specific command
workflow_position.downstream: Implementation artifacts, runbooks, and compatibility reports to session

analysis: Gap analysis document identifies root cause
design: Schema changes specified at file/function level
Migration path included for breaking changes
implementation: Integration tests pass for all satellite configurations
documentation: Migration runbook is clear and step-by-step
validation: Compatibility report covers all affected satellites

antipatterns: Skipping backward compatibility analysis (every change affects satellites)
Making schema changes without versioning strategy
examples.cross_team_protocol: Ecosystem-pack coordinates with all other packs. When escalating infrastructure changes to user, notify all active team leads.

frontmatter.description: Coordinates security phases for security work. Routes tasks through threat modeling, compliance, penetration testing, and review phases. Use when: security work spans multiple phases or requires cross-functional coordination. Triggers: coordinate, orchestrate, security workflow, security assessment, multi-phase security.

routing.threat-modeler: Security concern raised, threat model needed
routing.compliance-architect: Threat model complete, compliance requirements needed
routing.penetration-tester: Compliance design complete, security testing needed
routing.security-reviewer: Penetration testing complete, final review needed

workflow_position.downstream: Security signoff artifacts feed to deployment gates

threat-modeling: Threat model document complete with identified threats
penetration-testing: Vulnerabilities reported with severity and remediation

skills: @10x-workflow for complexity determination in security context
antipatterns: Treating PATCH as SYSTEM (different scope requires different phases)

frontmatter.description: Routes documentation work through audit, architecture, writing, and review phases. Use when: documentation spans multiple pages or requires structural planning. Triggers: coordinate, orchestrate, doc workflow, documentation planning.

architecture: Structure aligns with user mental models
